summary: Matrix recording captures a top‑notch 1989 show with standout new tunes like Victim, Built To Last, Blow Away and a solid Franklin’s Tower.
review: The concert is praised as a "top‑notch" performance, highlighted by an excellent first set of newer material (Victim, Built To Last, Blow Away) and a powerful second set that includes Death Don't Have No Mercy and a well‑paced nine‑minute Franklin's Tower. The matrix source delivers a balanced sound with no reported issues, making the show both musically and technically enjoyable.

ai_rating.rationale: Single detailed 5‑star review emphasizes strong performance and a good matrix recording; limited sample size reduces confidence to medium.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Matrix blend provides balanced, clear sound; reviewer did not note any specific audio flaws.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set delivers a strong run of new material; second set moves efficiently through heavy selections, keeping the energy high.
